summary: Full testing of 11-section ApplicationModal and /api/applications/full endpoint. All 11 form sections verified in UI. Backend endpoint saves all new fields correctly. Required field validation, China conditional display, and 4-step navigation all working. One backend bug found: admin GET /applications/{id} endpoint uses role=='admin' check but admin has role='admin_principal', causing 403.

minor.issue: Role check uses `role == 'admin'` but the admin account has role='admin_principal'. Admin gets 403 'Accès refusé' when trying to view individual applications by ID via this endpoint. Admin list works via /admin/applications but single-item GET does not.

issues: Payment amount shows '0 CNY' - the Data Science offer has no applicationFee set (falls to 0 instead of payment settings default). Currency mismatch: offer uses CNY but payment settings may use different currency. Check offer.fees.applicationFee configuration.

Suivant button is programmatically disabled (is_disabled()=True) before terms accepted, but visually appears fully blue (no visible opacity reduction). Tailwind disabled:opacity-50 may not be rendering as expected in some scenarios - UX feedback for disabled state is unclear.

action_items: Fix GET /api/applications/{app_id} role check: change `current_user.get('role') != 'admin'` to `current_user.get('role') not in ['admin', 'admin_principal', 'admin_secondary']` (or use the `get_admin_user` dependency)
Verify Data Science offer application fee is correctly set (shows 0 CNY on payment step)
Check if disabled:opacity-50 Tailwind class renders correctly for the Suivant button on Step 3 - add explicit opacity/color change for better UX disabled feedback
critical_code_review_comments: BUG: /api/applications/{app_id} (line 720 in _routes.py) checks `current_user.get('role') != 'admin'` but the admin system uses 'admin_principal' and 'admin_secondary' roles, not 'admin'. This means ADMINS CAN'T VIEW INDIVIDUAL APPLICATIONS VIA THIS ENDPOINT. Use `get_admin_user` dependency or check `role not in ['admin', 'admin_principal', 'admin_secondary']`
GOOD: All 11 new fields correctly modeled in ApplicationCreate Pydantic model with proper Optional types and defaults
GOOD: FullApplicationCreate = ApplicationCreate alias is clean and works correctly
GOOD: model_dump() usage in create_full_application correctly passes all new fields to Application model
GOOD: Application model uses ConfigDict(extra='ignore') which correctly ignores unknown fields

success_rate.frontend: 95% - All 11 sections verified, all 4 steps navigate correctly, China conditional display works, required field validation works

seed_data_creation: Created ui_test_user@test.com / Test2025! for UI testing (email verified via API). Also created several TEST_ prefixed users in backend tests for application submission testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 18: Full testing of 11-section ApplicationModal form. ApplicationModal.jsx completely rewritten with 11 sub-sections (1.Infos Personnelles, 2.Adresse Permanente, 3.Adresse Actuelle, 4.Etat Sante, 5.Chine, 6.Passeport, 7.Formation, 8.Experience, 9.Famille, 10.Garant, 11.Contact Urgence). Backend models extended with ~50 new fields. Main bug: /api/applications/{id} GET uses wrong role check (admin vs admin_principal). Test user created: ui_test_user@test.com / Test2025! (emailVerified=true). Backend test file: /app/backend/tests/test_full_application_form.py.

rca_of_issue.root_cause: Line 720 in /app/api/_routes.py checks `current_user.get('role') != 'admin'` but the admin system uses 'admin_principal' as the primary admin role (since line ~903 sets role to 'admin_principal'). The old check for 'admin' doesn't match the current role hierarchy.
rca_of_issue.reproduction: 1. Login as admin (admin@winners-consulting.com). 2. GET /api/applications/{any_app_id} with admin Bearer token. 3. Receives 403 'Accès refusé'.
rca_of_issue.mitigation: Change line 720 from `current_user.get('role') != 'admin'` to `current_user.get('role') not in ['admin', 'admin_principal', 'admin_secondary']` OR replace `Depends(get_current_user)` with `Depends(get_admin_user)` which already handles all admin roles.
